description: Letter in flowery Hebrew from Yiṣḥaq b. Benveniste of Narbonne, in Dimyāṭ (Damietta), who visited Egypt around 1105, writing to Yehoshuaʿ b. Dosa in Fustat asking for letters of recommendation to the government in order to be able to continue traveling. Mentions the Fatimid vizier al-Malik al-Afḍal (r. 1094–1121), Tripoli, Jabla, and the lands of Ishmael and Edom (possibly Byzantium), to which Yiṣḥaq was hoping to travel, and reports that at nighttime he was robbed and needed 8 dinars to replace his good clothing. Address in Judaeo-Arabic on verso. (Information in part CUDL and in part from Mediterranean Society vol. II, 131) Join: Oded Zinger 10/18/25
description_he: מכתב בעברית פרחונית מיצחק בן בנבנישטי מנרבון, דמיאט, כ-1105, ליהושע בן דוסה בפוסטאט: מבקש מכתבי המלצה לממשל. מוזכר הוויזיר אל-מלך אל-אפדל. נשדד בלילה וצריך 8 דינר לחלוף בגדים.
